# 62 Essential Law Firm KPIs and Performance Metrics for Attorneys

62 Essential Law Firm KPIs and Performance Metrics for Attorneys**

62 Essential Law Firm KPIs and Performance Metrics for Attorneys

![An illustration of attorneys holding up law firm KPIs](https://www.clio.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/law-firm-KPIs-750x422.jpeg "An illustration of attorneys holding up law firm KPIs")
![An illustration of attorneys holding up law firm KPIs](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAP///wAAACH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAICRAEAOw== "An illustration of attorneys holding up law firm KPIs")

As a lawyer, you’re an expert on the practice of law—but are you taking the [time to track](https://www.clio.com/features/legal-time-expense-tracking/) what’s important when it comes to guiding the success of your firm? **Law firm KPIs**, or Key Performance Indicators, can help. They can help you answer questions like:

Monitoring the performance of pivotal aspects of your firm can make a major impact on your firm’s [revenues](https://www.clio.com/blog/average-law-firm-revenue/), [growth](https://www.clio.com/blog/growing-a-law-firm/), and overall success. But to succeed in our changing legal landscape, you must evolve what and how you measure.

By planning, selecting, measuring, and analyzing law firm KPIs specific to your business, you’ll be better able to make [data-driven decisions](https://www.clio.com/blog/data-driven-lawyer/ "data driven lawyer") and informed changes to improve your practice.

In the following guide, we’ll explore how using law firm KPIs impacts your law firm’s success, and how to pick the right KPIs for your practice’s unique situation. We’ve also assembled a vast list of potential law firm KPIs to manage—so you’ll have plenty of ideas to bring back to your firm.

## Why are law firm KPIs valuable?

Nowadays, you hear every entrepreneur and investor saying “KPI”—so much that you might think of “KPI” as merely a buzzword. But what exactly are KPIs, and why are they important?

Key Performance Indicators, or just “KPIs,” are business metrics that evaluate an organization’s performance and success. KPIs give you the data and knowledge to answer critical questions, and they should be measurable, specific, and targeted to the unique goals of a business.

By learning to pinpoint, measure, and leverage specific performance indicators, law firm KPIs provide:

### Measurable performance

There’s a big difference between simply collecting data and analyzing data. If you want to use data to determine if you’ve performed well or not, you need to first identify what you’re trying to achieve. By identifying and tracking goals for performance indicators (for example, the KPI of monthly revenue), you can better assess how your firm is performing.

### Accountability

If you haven’t identified what KPIs to track, it’s difficult to determine who or what is responsible for wins or losses at your firm—which in turn makes it difficult to replicate or avoid certain results. Selecting and monitoring specific performance indicators creates a clean line of accountability at your firm.

### Predictability

Without setting and tracking KPIs, you’re often left to speculate over why things are going wrong (or right) within your firm. With KPIs, you can track specifics over time, making it easier to predict results for what will work in the future.

### Easier tracking

Once you’ve established a system for monitoring your law firm KPIs, upkeep is relatively simple. It can be as easy as assigning a staff member to input the numbers for each predetermined KPI once a month. Then, you can set aside a block of time for yourself to analyze the results, identify patterns (good and bad), and find opportunities for adjustment.

When using a practice management software like Clio to track all of your cases, contacts, and billing in one place, one of the advantages is that it’s really easy to run reports to get the information you need to track your most important KPIs.

## 62 law firm KPIs and performance metrics to track

It’s true what they say: What gets measured, gets managed.

Now that it’s clear why [data-driven](https://www.clio.com/blog/data-driven-improve-legal-practice/ "data-driven ways to improve your legal practice") KPIs are critical to success, read on for our list of KPI examples for lawyers to measure and manage at your firm (Note: for each KPI, set a predetermined time period, i.e., per previous month or year, in which to keep track).

Whichever law firm KPIs you choose to use, it’s important to keep in mind that none of these metrics mean anything out of context. The type of practice, firm, and lawyer lifestyle objectives all factor into setting the targets to evaluate these numbers.

### Law firm marketing metrics

Businesses and law firms alike fail because they pay out more than they bring in. Measuring more than the traditional billable hour metrics can help your firm better understand its current and potential monetary situation.

Here are a few marketing KPIs to consider:

### Client acquisition KPIs

New clients are vital to the success of your law firm—but finding them is a complex process.

Consider using some of the following client acquisition KPIs to show you what’s working for your firm:

### Client development KPIs

Once you have a client, what do you do to develop your relationship with them? How efficiently are you resolving their cases?

To get a better understanding of your client base, try adding some of the following client development KPIs to your assessment roster:

### Client satisfaction KPIs

Measuring client satisfaction is a new concept for the legal industry, but knowing what you can improve upon—based on solid feedback—is critical to business success, today.

Here are some performance indicators to help you evaluate client satisfaction:

### Law firm productivity metrics

An efficient law firm is a profitable law firm—yet productivity is a huge issue for most, with legal professionals billing an average of less than three hours a day, according to the latest *[Legal Trend Report](https://www.clio.com/resources/legal-trends/ "Legal Trends Report")*.

By [tracking your firm’s productivity](https://www.clio.com/blog/tracking-productivity-law-firm/ "tracking productivity at a mid sized firm"), you can locate areas where time is being wasted. By doing so, you can identify areas where time can be redirected to revenue-creating opportunities.

Here are some law firm KPIs to consider when it comes to productivity:

If you’re using [Clio Manage](https://www.clio.com/manage/ "Clio Manage"), the [Firm Dashboard](https://www.clio.com/features/law-firm-insights/) captures and tracks your firm’s utilization, realization, and collection rates for you—putting these KPIs at your fingertips so you can quickly and regularly gain insights and track productivity for your firm.

*Check out our blog showcasing the [top law firm business intelligence tools.](https://www.clio.com/blog/law-firm-business-intelligence-software/)*

### Individual legal performance metrics

While law firm KPIs are useful for measuring the overall success and growth of your firm, you might also want to track performance for individual members of your team—whether for yourself or for staff members. These individual performance KPIs could include metrics like:

### Financial KPIs

Of course, financials, profitability, and margins are central to the success or failure of your law firm—so pay special attention to the following list of financial KPIs:

You can also look at your average:

### Firm culture KPIs

When looking at your law firm KPIs, you might want to consider looking beyond revenues and clients, and looking inwards to your firm culture.

Firm culture KPIs can offer valuable insights on the health of your organization that might otherwise be overlooked—which is important, as office culture can have an impact on clients, and thus your overall business.

## How to choose the best KPIs for your attorney practice

It’s all well and good to know that you want to [measure performance to assess your firm’s strengths](https://www.clio.com/blog/law-firm-kpis-how-well-law-firm-doing/ "law firm KPIs"), but—with seemingly endless areas to assess—how do you decide which specific KPIs are most valuable for your law firm?

Remember that not all KPIs are useful for your firm. Trying to measure every performance indicator available is overwhelming, needlessly time-consuming, and counterproductive. With this in mind—before you look at which firm-specific KPIs you want to measure—you should first think about your practice’s unique overall goals, and how your firm measures success.

Here are a few questions to consider before looking at specific KPIs:

### 1. What do you need to measure?

When it comes to metrics you can measure, the possibilities are endless. Start by deciding what type of information provides valuable insight into your business’s performance and what data is needed in order to give you that information.

### 2. What data do you already have?

You can’t measure what you don’t track. The first step in establishing your KPIs is looking at the data you already have available in your firm’s practice management system.

### 3. What are your targets?

Are you doing criminal defense work where you hope that the client is only with you once? Or are you doing intellectual property work that can be repeated over the years? What is the lifetime value of every client?

Every firm has to set its own targets before choosing KPIs to measure, because they are unique to your practice. For example, your target for the cost of client acquisition depends on the type of law and the return on investment per client.

## 6 6 Steps to effectively track law firm KPIs

Now that you have a few ideas for which KPIs you want to monitor at your firm, what do you need to do to actually use them to your advantage?

Try adopting the old management model of “Plan, Do, Check, Act.” Plan to gather the data for your KPIs. Once you’ve completed and analyzed these KPIs, take corrective action and then repeat this monthly.

Essentially, you want to follow these six steps:

## Conclusion

Integrating KPIs into your business planning allows you to create a customized dashboard for monitoring and evolving the success of your law firm. By setting specific targets and then tracking and measuring key performance indicators, you can empower yourself to make more effective decisions on behalf of your law firm, clients, and staff.

## What is KPI in law?

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | A KPI—Key Performance Indicator—is a measurable goal that law firms use to assess their performance. For example, common KPIs in law include percentage of cases won, client satisfaction, billable hours, and realization rate. Focusing on clearly-defined KPIs ensures law firms focus on what truly matters. |
|  |  |
|  |  |

## How do you measure success in a law firm?

Law firms can measure success in several ways. Some firms might consider revenue the most important metric, while others may focus more heavily on client satisfaction/retention or the number of cases won. However, these aren’t mutually exclusive—they’re all good markers of law firm success.

## How do law firms measure productivity?

There are several ways law firms measure productivity. These might include the number of hours billed, revenue generated per year, the number of cases resolved, the utilization rate per attorney, the speed and accuracy of document preparation, the number of new clients acquired, or the firm’s overall profitability.

## What are some common KPIs for measuring law firm efficiency?

Common KPIs for law firm efficiency include billable hours, utilization rates, and case completion times. These metrics help firms gauge how effectively they are using their resources and identify areas where processes can be streamlined to improve overall productivity.

## What are the benefits of benchmarking KPIs against industry standards for law firms?

Benchmarking KPIs against industry standards allows law firms to assess their performance relative to competitors. This comparison helps identify strengths and weaknesses, guiding firms in making strategic improvements and maintaining a competitive edge in the legal market.

## What role do technology KPIs play in a law firm?

Technology KPIs, such as system uptime and software adoption rates, help law firms assess the effectiveness of their tech infrastructure. By monitoring these metrics, firms can ensure their technology supports efficient operations and make necessary upgrades to maintain competitiveness.

--- SOURCE 2: KPIs for Legal Teams: How Tech GCs Measure Success | The L Suite ---
URL: https://www.lsuite.co/blog/kpis-for-legal-teams

SUMMARY:
Legal KPIs vary from organization to organization but often include metrics like contract turnaround times, legal spend as it relates to company

FULL CONTENT:
# KPIs for Legal Teams: How Tech GCs Measure Success

![Jotting notes in breakout session at Fullstack GC Conference]()

Experienced GCs share expert insights on defining and measuring legal KPIs — and why it's important to track them in the first place.

Authors

Desirina Frew

Senior Manager, Content & Social Marketing

Team Building & Management

Featuring Insights From:

## People

### Roman Perchyts

![]()

### Clarissa Cerda

![]()

### Roxanne Quinlan

![]()

### Jeri Looney

![]()

### René Paula

![René Paula, Chief Legal Officer & Head of Corp Dev, at NotCo]()

### Matt Boisen

![]()

While CFOs might not be fluent in legalese and GCs may be stymied by sales jargon, there's one language every business stakeholder understands: Cold, hard KPIs (Key Performance Indicators).

KPIs can be a lingua franca that bridges sales, finance, product, and more. Concrete metrics that demonstrate success not only streamline [cross-departmental collaboration](https://www.lsuite.co/blog/top-gcs-share-their-strategies-for-partnering-with-marketing-on-consumer-privacy-issues), but also take the ambiguity out of a legal team's value.

Below, L Suite members make the case for setting legal KPIs, specific metrics to track, and common challenges to navigate.

### **Key Takeaways:**

Tracking KPIs allows legal teams to quantify their impact on the business, demonstrating value beyond traditional legal functions.

Legal KPIs vary from organization to organization but often include metrics like contract turnaround times, legal spend as it relates to company revenue, and speed of deal closures.

Challenges around tracking KPIs include getting organizational buy-in and the logistics of collecting data. Ensuring clear, consistent communication with all stakeholders and adopting software solutions can help mitigate these pain points.

## The Case for KPIs

Analyzing metrics related to a legal function's volume of work, complexity of tasks, and outcomes can help GCs make data-driven decisions about where to allocate resources — as well as when to automate processes, adopt new tools, or [make new hires](https://www.lsuite.co/blog/general-counsel-guide-when-to-hire-second-lawyer). It can also ensure the rest of the organization views legal as a strategic business partner invested in the company's larger goals and success.

As one L Suite member puts it, KPI metrics typically fall into three broad categories:

How legal helps the company make money

How it helps the company make more money more efficiently over time

How it helps the company save money

More specifically, KPIs encompass everything from contract turnaround times to compliance and risk — though the specific KPIs an organization focuses on will vary depending on the company's industry and stage of growth.

## Key KPIs for Legal Departments to Track

In an L Suite Braintrust conversation, [Roman Perchyts](https://www.linkedin.com/in/roman-perchyts/), General Counsel at document workflow platform airSlate explained that he breaks legal KPIs into four categories:

Time (e.g. contract turnaround time, time to respond to an HR request)

Quantity (e.g. number of requests processed per teammate, number of requests month over month)

Quality (e.g., Net Promoter Scores, satisfaction survey results, and risk scores)

Money (cost per contract, spend against budget, spend over time, etc.)

Within those buckets, specific KPIs may include:

**Legal Spend vs. Revenue**: This metric provides insight into how efficiently legal services are delivered and the impact of legal spend on the company's bottom line. Tracking this ratio over time helps the business make informed decisions about where to invest in legal capabilities — like when to hire or ramp up use of outside counsel.

**Contract-Related KPIs**: By analyzing contract throughput and bottlenecks, legal departments can identify areas for improvement or automation — often leading to faster deal cycles. [Clarissa Cerda](https://www.linkedin.com/in/clarissa-cerda), Chief Legal Officer at information security company Pindrop Security, Inc., noted that measuring contract metrics involves a degree of finesse. "The nuance is how you measure and whether you're keeping a contract log that helps you track turnaround time or time-to-close average once it hits legal queue," she wrote in a recent L Suite conversation on Braintrust.

**Non-Contract-Related KPIs**: Perchyts wrote that one of the main priorities when identifying KPIs is tracking not only contract performance, but the efficacy of the entire legal function. To do this, he monitors metrics like:

Percentage spent vs budget

Percentage of quarterly Objectives and Key Results (OKR) completion

Internal team satisfaction rating, based on quarterly or bi-annual anonymous feedback surveys

Number of legal claims over time (where legal claims are indicative of risk)

Average number of days for processing requests (where speed is important)

Risk scoring of contracts, claims, and programs (like privacy or security) over time

Simple yes/no responses on certain self-evident projects like fundraising or M&A

## A Note on KPIs vs. OKRs

In The L Suite community, there's some debate on the difference between KPIs versus OKRs. Some companies opt for one over the other, but both concepts involve setting goals and measuring outcomes.

While KPIs are crucial for ongoing performance monitoring and operational efficiency, OKRs are valuable for setting and achieving growth-oriented goals. KPIs can indicate success or failure in specific areas by offering continuous monitoring over time, while OKRs are ambitious, specific objectives within a set timeframe (often quarters or fiscal years).

[Roxanne Quinlan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/roxanne-quinlan?originalSubdomain=au), General Counsel at marketplace platform and dropshipping solution Marketplacer, tends to use OKRs to motivate the team to complete important projects within a set timeframe. “For example, we measure our privacy maturity score against an independent standard. An OKR might be to improve our score from X to Y by X date. Sitting behind that is work required to strengthen the privacy program," she said.

## Implementing KPIs: Common Challenges and Potential Solutions

Defining and tracking legal KPIs isn't without its challenges, from selecting meaningful metrics to ensuring accurate data. Here's what L Suite members have to say about overcoming these hurdles.

### Defining the 'Right' KPIs

Every organization has a different approach to KPIs, and what works in one environment may not translate to another. One area where this is particularly evident is in measuring turnaround times for contracts. As any GC knows, the root cause of a delay is often multifaceted, as many different parties bear a degree of responsibility.

"[Metrics] re: contract turnaround time can come back to haunt you unless you have very standard, non-negotiated agreements and are essentially just processing," wrote [Jeri Looney](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeri-rouse-looney-7670815/), former General Counsel at hydrogen-electric aviation company ZeroAvia. "One big, nasty negotiation and your turnaround time can get blown out of the water."

[René Paula](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rpaula/), Chief Legal Officer at AI company NotCo, suggests looking at the bigger picture. "To elevate the legal function to a strategic level, you need to have KPIs that are not about the sausage-making, but rather major transactions closed, new markets opened, litigations settled or won, or dollars saved by renegotiating major deals/leases," he wrote. "I try to stay away from ever being in a slide deck that says something like, 'The help desk cleared 2,000 tickets this month.' That's not what a board of directors cares about, so neither should you."

### Collecting and Analyzing Data

A KPI is only as effective as your ability to measure it — a task that can be complicated by gaps in legal technology infrastructure or clunky integration with other business systems. To streamline data collection and tracking, some L Suite members have adopted software solutions that allow their teams to monitor key metrics like the number/types of deals they're working on and current stage of completion.

In addition to facilitating easier housekeeping, software can be immensely helpful when it comes to pinpointing bottlenecks. Tracking platforms can illuminate details like how much time a contract is sitting with legal vs. another business unit vs. a counterparty, which can help put a stop to unproductive "blame games."

Some GCs have gone the bespoke route for tracking the status of legal team tasks, asking IT to build out a simple "legal department task tracker" that keeps track of when a task started, what its due date was, and when it finished.

### Getting Organizational Buy-In

Implementing new processes can be met with resistance. This may stem from concerns within the legal department about being reduced to mere numbers, or from other departments wary of additional demands on their time and resources.

Active engagement with all stakeholders is a must for boosting buy-in. Demonstrating the benefits of KPIs through pilot programs can provide tangible examples of their value, helping dispel fears or doubts. Additionally, being open to feedback and willing to adapt KPI strategies can encourage a sense of ownership and collaboration.

And sometimes, results speak for themselves. When [Matt Boisen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattboisen/), General Counsel at SaaS platform Highspot first joined the company, he found a convoluted, manual agreement process was bogging down deal closures. "Every agreement was being heavily negotiated, which wasn't scalable," he said. To streamline the process, Boisen and his team did some benchmarking and ultimately landed on a strategy to standardize order forms.

This move wasn't fully embraced by the sales team at first — but three years after implementing the standardization strategy, Highspot has transitioned from completing just 8% of deals via order form to 75%. The move has significantly streamlined the deal-closure process, and sales is now firmly in favor.

Boisen's team's ability to showcase concrete progress boosted confidence in the legal team across the whole organization. "We now have a report that shows the percentage of contracts that were order forms and how long it takes to get deals done," he said. "It helps the business understand that legal isn't just a black box where contracts go and pop out signed two months later."

*For expert insights on how to start defining and tracking KPIs, L Suite members can be a valuable resource. [Apply to join](https://www.lsuite.co/apply) the community today.*

# 10 Legal Department KPIs to Track in 2024

###### 

Track 10 essential KPIs for legal departments in 2024 to optimize operations and drive continuous improvement. Measure contract volume, task turnaround time, legal spend, compliance, litigation support, efficiency, technology adoption, client satisfaction, project progress, and employee engagement.

## Save 90% on your legal bills

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/65f106bfe7e25afdb317678f/664d70e59c73f9195f5118c5_664d4acaa353cbfc95764d99-4064ef2538e2af04beed014aa03244c7.jpeg)

Measuring performance is crucial for legal teams to evaluate their work, identify areas for improvement, and demonstrate their value. However, quantifying legal work can be challenging due to its qualitative nature. By tracking the following 10 essential KPIs, legal departments can effectively measure their performance:

`- Conduct employee satisfaction surveys
- Monitor turnover rates and training participation
- Implement strategies to foster career growth and work-life balance`

By consistently tracking these KPIs, legal departments can gain insights, optimize operations, and drive continuous improvement, ensuring they stay ahead in today's competitive business landscape.

## Related video from YouTube

## 1. Contract Volume and Complexity

Keeping track of the number and complexity of contracts is crucial for legal teams. This helps them understand their workload and allocate resources properly.

### Tracking Contract Numbers

Measuring the number of contracts handled by the legal department provides insights into the team's workload. This metric can help:

Tracking contract numbers also allows legal teams to assess their productivity and efficiency, helping them identify areas for process improvement.

### Categorizing Contract Complexity

Contracts vary in complexity. Some are relatively simple, while others are highly complex, requiring more time and effort from the legal team. Categorizing contracts based on their complexity can help legal departments:

A common approach is to categorize contracts as "Simple," "Moderate," or "Complex" based on factors such as the number of clauses, the level of negotiation required, or the involvement of third parties.

### Monitoring Contract Value

In addition to volume and complexity, tracking the value of contracts can provide valuable insights into the legal department's involvement in high-value transactions. This metric can help:

| Benefit | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Demonstrate contribution | Show the legal team's contribution to the organization's revenue and profitability |
| Identify high-stakes contracts | Recognize contracts that may require additional scrutiny or resources |
| Prioritize efforts | Prioritize contract review and negotiation efforts based on potential financial impact |

## 2. Task Turnaround Time

### Measuring Task Completion

Tracking how long it takes to finish legal tasks is key to finding delays and inefficiencies in your processes. By monitoring metrics like:

Legal teams can understand their productivity and workload management better.

Measuring task completion times helps:

### Identifying Process Bottlenecks

Analyzing turnaround times across different legal work types can reveal process bottlenecks and areas for optimization. For instance, if contract reviews consistently take longer than expected, it could indicate a need for streamlining the review process or using contract automation tools.

By breaking down turnaround times into specific phases or tasks, legal teams can:

### Excluding External Factors

When measuring task turnaround times, it's essential to exclude external factors beyond the legal department's control. For example, delays caused by waiting for business approvals or third-party responses should be accounted for separately.

By isolating the time spent solely on legal tasks, teams can:

| Benefit | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Assess internal efficiency | Gain a clear picture of the department's productivity without external influences |
| Identify process optimization areas | Focus on improving internal processes and workflows within their control |
| Set realistic SLAs | Establish achievable turnaround targets based on the department's actual capabilities |

Regularly monitoring task turnaround times and excluding external factors provides legal teams with a data-driven approach to continuously improve their processes, enhance efficiency, and better serve their internal clients.

## 3. Legal Spend vs. Revenue

### Total Legal Costs

Tracking both internal and external legal costs is crucial to understand the full financial impact of legal operations. Internal costs include salaries, benefits, technology, and operational expenses for in-house legal staff. External costs cover fees paid to outside counsel, litigation support vendors, and other third-party legal service providers.

Monitoring total legal costs holistically allows organizations to:

### Legal Spend as Percentage of Revenue

To enable meaningful comparisons across organizations of different sizes and industries, legal departments should calculate their total legal spend as a percentage of company revenue. This metric provides a standardized view of legal costs relative to the organization's financial performance.

By tracking legal spend as a percentage of revenue, teams can:

| Benefit | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Evaluate cost-efficiency | Assess whether legal spending is proportionate to the company's revenue and identify potential areas for optimization. |
| Set performance targets | Establish goals for reducing legal costs as a percentage of revenue, driving continuous improvement. |
| Facilitate data-driven decision-making | Use this metric to justify budget allocations, resource investments, or process improvements. |
| Enable industry benchmarking | Compare legal spend ratios against industry peers or best-in-class organizations to identify gaps or opportunities. |

### Internal vs. External Spend

Monitoring the ratio of internal legal spending (e.g., salaries, technology) to external legal spending (e.g., outside counsel fees) can reveal opportunities for cost savings and efficiency gains. A high reliance on external legal services may indicate the need for expanding in-house capabilities or optimizing processes to reduce external dependencies.

By tracking internal vs. external legal spend, organizations can:

Regularly analyzing these key legal spend metrics empowers organizations to make data-driven decisions, optimize resource allocation, and drive cost-effectiveness within their legal operations.

## 4. Compliance and Risk Management

Tracking compliance and risk is vital for legal teams. It helps ensure rules are followed and potential legal issues are addressed before they become costly problems.

### Monitoring Compliance

Legal teams should track these compliance metrics:

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Compliance Training Completion Rate | Percentage of employees who completed mandatory compliance training. Comprehensive training promotes a culture of compliance. |
| Compliance Resource Allocation | Resources dedicated to compliance efforts, including staff, technology, and budgets. Adequate resources are crucial for an effective compliance program. |

### Managing Risk

Effective risk management protects the organization from legal liabilities and financial losses. Consider these risk metrics:

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Risk Mitigation Training Effectiveness | Measure the effectiveness of risk mitigation training by tracking employee participation, knowledge retention, and reduction in risk incidents. |
| Risk Mitigation Strategy Success Rate | Monitor the success rate of implemented risk mitigation strategies, such as compliance programs, alternative dispute resolution, and risk transfer initiatives (e.g., insurance coverage). |

## 5. Litigation Support

Tracking legal cases is vital for managing risks, costs, and outcomes. Here are key metrics to monitor:

### Litigation Case Tracking

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Active Cases | Total ongoing litigation matters across the organization. |
| Average Case Cost | Average cost per case, including legal fees, court costs, and settlements/judgments. |
| Case Resolution Time | Average time taken to resolve a case, from start to finish. |
| Favorable Outcomes | Percentage of cases resolved in favor of the organization. |

### Analyzing Litigation Data

Analyzing case data helps identify trends, areas for improvement, and better litigation strategies:

###### sbb-itb-ea3f94f

## 6. Efficiency and Productivity

Measuring how well a legal team works is key. By tracking key numbers, teams can find areas to improve, streamline processes, and show their value.

### Performance Metrics

To gauge efficiency and productivity, track these metrics:

1. **Matters per Attorney**: The number of legal matters each attorney handles in a given time. This shows workload and resource use.

2. **Legal vs. Admin Tasks**: Time spent by attorneys on legal work versus admin tasks. Aim to minimize time on non-billable admin tasks.

3. **High-Value Work Time**: Time attorneys spend on high-value tasks like legal advice, risk reduction, and revenue-generating work. Ensure legal resources focus on critical, value-adding tasks.

4. **Deadline Compliance**: Percentage of legal tasks completed on time. Timely work is crucial for client satisfaction and minimizing legal risks.

5. **Cost per Matter**: Average cost of handling different legal matters. Identify cost optimization opportunities and better resource allocation.

### Process Improvement

Continuously analyze these metrics to find ways to improve processes, automate tasks, and better allocate resources:

1. **Streamline Workflows**: Look for bottlenecks and inefficiencies in existing workflows. Implement improvements like automating repetitive tasks, using legal tech, and optimizing team collaboration.

2. **Use Technology**: Adopt legal tech solutions to enhance productivity and efficiency. Tools like contract management software, e-billing systems, and document automation can significantly reduce manual work and improve turnaround times.

3. **Optimize Resources**: Based on workload, ensure attorneys focus on high-value tasks aligned with their expertise. Consider outsourcing or using alternative legal service providers for routine or low-risk matters.

4. **Follow Best Practices**: Research and implement industry best practices for legal operations. Collaborate with peers, attend conferences, and stay updated on trends and technologies for continuous improvement.

5. **Collaborate**: Encourage collaboration between the legal team and other business units. Effective communication and alignment can streamline processes, reduce redundancies, and enhance overall organizational efficiency.

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Matters per Attorney | Number of legal matters handled by each attorney within a given period. |
| Legal vs. Admin Tasks | Ratio of time spent by attorneys on legal work versus administrative tasks. |
| High-Value Work Time | Time dedicated by attorneys to high-value activities like strategic legal advice and revenue-generating initiatives. |
| Deadline Compliance | Percentage of legal tasks and deliverables completed within agreed-upon timelines. |
| Cost per Matter | Average cost associated with handling different types of legal matters. |

## 7. Legal Technology Adoption

Using legal technology tools is key for boosting efficiency and [streamlining legal processes](https://www.cimphony.ai/insights/automated-legal-forms-streamlining-operations). By tracking how these tools are implemented and used, legal teams can measure their impact and keep improving.

### Tracking Tool Usage

To ensure legal tech tools are adopted successfully and provide maximum benefits, it's crucial to monitor how they're being used:

1. **Adoption Rates**: What percentage of legal staff actively uses the new technology? Low adoption may mean more training or user experience improvements are needed.

2. **User Activity**: How often are users logging in, uploading documents, or interacting with workflows? High activity suggests the tools are well-integrated into daily work.

3. **Feature Usage**: Which tool features are used most? This data can guide future enhancements, training, or prioritizing specific functions.

4. **User Feedback**: Regularly collect feedback from users through surveys, groups, or direct communication. This qualitative input reveals user experiences, pain points, and areas for improvement.

### Measuring Efficiency Gains

Implementing legal tech should ultimately lead to increased efficiency and cost savings. To measure the impact, track:

| Metric | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Turnaround Time | Average time to complete legal tasks before and after using new tech. |
| Cost per Matter | Compare average costs for handling legal matters, factoring in tech costs and potential savings. |
| Billable Hours | Track billable hours for attorneys, aiming to reduce non-billable admin tasks. |
| Workflow Automation | Percentage of manual tasks automated through legal tech. |
| Resource Allocation | Ability to shift legal resources to higher-value tasks due to increased efficiency and automation. |

## 8. Client Satisfaction

Keeping clients happy is key for any legal team. Happy clients are more likely to use your services again and recommend you to others.

### Getting Client Feedback

One way to check if clients are satisfied is by sending out surveys. These surveys can be sent after finishing a legal matter or on a regular schedule (like every quarter).

When making surveys, keep these tips in mind:

### Tracking Important Numbers

Along with feedback, legal teams should track numbers that show client satisfaction, like:

| Metric | What it Measures |
| --- | --- |
| Response Time | How quickly you respond to client requests, showing responsiveness. |
| Resolution Time | How long it takes to resolve legal matters, showing efficiency. |
| Repeat Business | Percentage of clients who use your services again, indicating satisfaction. |
| Referral Rate | Number of clients who recommend you to others, suggesting positive experiences. |
| Perceived Value | Client ratings on the quality of your legal advice or services, reflecting overall satisfaction. |

## 9. Strategic Project Progress

### Identifying Key Milestones

To track progress on strategic legal projects, it's important to identify key milestones and deliverables. These milestones act as checkpoints to evaluate if the project is on track and aligned with overall goals.

Some examples of key milestones include:

Regularly monitoring progress against these milestones helps identify potential delays early on and take corrective actions to keep the project on schedule.

### Using Project Management Tools

Project management tools and techniques can help track strategic project progress effectively. Legal teams should consider adopting project management software tailored to their needs. These tools can:

| Feature | Benefit |
| --- | --- |
| Define project scope, timelines, and resource allocation | Provides a clear roadmap for the project |
| Assign tasks and responsibilities to team members | Ensures accountability and efficient collaboration |
| Monitor task completion and project deadlines | Keeps the project on track and identifies potential delays |
| Facilitate collaboration and communication among stakeholders | Promotes transparency and effective teamwork |
| Generate progress reports and visualize project data | Offers insights into project status and performance |

Popular project management tools used in legal departments include [Trello](https://trello.com/), [Asana](https://asana.com/), and [Jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira). Legal-specific solutions like [SimpleLegal](https://www.simplelegal.com/) and [LegalTrek](https://legaltrek.com/) also offer project management capabilities designed for legal workflows.

## 10. Employee Engagement and Retention

### Measuring Employee Satisfaction

Keeping employees happy and engaged is vital for a high-performing legal team. Monitor these key metrics:

### Promoting a Positive Work Environment

Retaining top legal talent requires proactive strategies to foster career growth, work-life balance, and a positive workplace culture:

| Strategy | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Career Development Programs** | Implement structured career paths, mentorship, and training tailored to legal professionals' needs, demonstrating commitment to their long-term growth. |
| **Flexible Work Options** | Offer remote work, flexible schedules, and job-sharing to support work-life balance and accommodate diverse lifestyle needs. |
| **Recognition and Rewards** | Establish formal programs to recognize and reward outstanding performance, achievements, and contributions from legal staff. Public recognition boosts morale and engagement. |
| **Wellness Initiatives** | Prioritize employee well-being by providing mental health resources, stress management workshops, and encouraging work-life balance. Address the unique challenges of the legal profession. |
| **Collaborative Culture** | Foster an inclusive environment where legal professionals feel valued, respected, and empowered to contribute ideas. Encourage open communication and teamwork. |

## Conclusion

### Key Takeaways

In today's competitive business world, legal teams play a vital role in ensuring organizational success. By tracking the right Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), legal departments can:

This guide covered 10 essential KPIs that legal departments should consider monitoring in 2024:

### Using Data-Driven Metrics

Adopting a data-driven approach to performance measurement is crucial for legal departments to stay ahead. By consistently monitoring and analyzing relevant KPIs, legal teams can:

Regular KPI tracking enables legal departments to identify trends, uncover inefficiencies, and proactively address potential issues.

### Tailoring KPIs to Organizational Needs

While this guide provides an overview of essential legal department KPIs, every organization has unique goals, challenges, and priorities. Legal teams should tailor their KPI selection and monitoring processes to align with their organization's specific objectives and legal department's strategic goals. By customizing KPIs to their unique needs, legal departments can ensure they are measuring the metrics that truly matter and driving meaningful impact within their organization.

| KPI | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| Contract Volume and Complexity | Ensure efficient contract management |
| Task Turnaround Time | Identify bottlenecks and streamline processes |
| Legal Spend vs. Revenue | Optimize legal spend and resource allocation |
| Compliance and Risk Management | Maintain regulatory adherence and minimize legal exposure |
| Litigation Support | Identify trends and opportunities for proactive risk management |
| Efficiency and Productivity | Enhance overall legal department productivity |
| Legal Technology Adoption | Measure efficiency gains from technology implementation |
| Client Satisfaction | Ensure high client satisfaction with legal services |
| Strategic Project Progress | Track progress of strategic legal initiatives |
| Employee Engagement and Retention | Retain top legal talent and foster a positive work environment |

### How to measure the performance of a legal department?

Legal departments can measure their performance using various Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). Here are some common KPIs:

1. **Total Legal Costs**

2. **Legal Costs as a Percentage of Revenue**

3. **Budget Adherence**

| --- | --- |

4. **In-House vs. Outside Counsel Ratio**

6. **Cost per Lawyer**

# Demonstrating the value of in-house Legal: KPIs and metrics that matter

18 December 2024

More than ten years have passed since [Bain & Company](https://www.bain.com/insights/a-higher-bar) highlighted a perception gap: while 40% of CLOs believed their legal teams drove commercial value, only 14% of CEOs shared that view. Today, bridging this gap remains an ongoing challenge for legal teams.”

The most effective way for in-house Legal departments to demonstrate their value is to apply data-driven [Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)](https://www.plexus.co/learn/legal-transformation/kpis) as it is universally used and understood by the broader business. By adopting [agile methodologies](https://www.plexus.co/learn/legal-transformation/agile-legal) to execute strategic objectives, value can easily be measured and communicated. Here’s how to do it.

## Agility: The need for speed

[Gartner predicts](https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2024-11-13-gartner-predicts-legal-risk-and-compliance-functions-to-double-technology-spend-by-2027) that by 2027, Legal, Risk and Compliance functions will double their spend on technology, highlighting the ongoing shift toward data-driven decision-making. These tools have not only streamlined workflows but are also providing actionable insights that enhance operational transparency and strategic alignment.

Technology is giving glacial teams a big boost in becoming more agile, while continuing to accelerate the fastest adopters. By using workflow automation and productivity tools, agile legal teams generate data as a byproduct of daily operations, enhancing efficiency and enabling real-time performance measurement. This adaptability ensures they meet business demands effectively while reducing operational friction.

Agile legal functions are evolving from operational bottlenecks into strategic enablers. As [McKinsey predicts](https://www.imd.org/research-knowledge/disruption/articles/why-you-will-probably-live-longer-than-most-big-companies), 75% of current S&P 500 companies may drop off the index by 2027, delivering a wake-up call for legal operations to be agile – lest they lose their standing.

## It’s a numbers game

Previously, law firms and legal teams often highlighted their impact through anecdotal evidence and qualitative assessments. While these narratives still hold value, today’s business environment relies solely on data-driven insights to demonstrate effectiveness. KPIs such as legal spend as a percentage of company revenue, turnaround times, and client satisfaction scores (also known as Net Promoter Score, NPS for short) directly measure legal performance against business outcomes.

For example, a legal team’s ability to work on high-risk, high-value matters without increasing headcount demonstrates efficiently scalable contributions that resonate with strategic goals. This indisputable data showcases Legal’s value and helps with buy-in from the business to advance both departmental and organisational objectives.

## Defining your North Star metric

To redefine Legal’s strategic role, teams must adopt a North Star Metric — this can be expressed through a simple formula:

![Plexus Legal Value Formula](https://www.plexus.co/images/Screen-Shot-2020-06-11-at-10.24.02-am.png)

[Legal Drag](https://www.plexus.co/resources/articles/legal-industry-disruption) is defined as ​“the extent to which Legal’s involvement impedes the generation of revenue or efficient execution of strategy”. By reducing drag, total Legal Value increases.

By aligning metrics with business outcomes, Legal can shift perceptions from being cost centers to strategic value creators, fostering stronger partnerships across the enterprise.

## Five key KPIs for Legal teams

In-house legal teams across different industries may have a variety of objective focuses, but KPIs can serve as a universal compass to navigate challenges and deliver value towards reaching the North Star metric. By tracking these data points, teams can demonstrate their contribution to operational efficiency and strategic alignment. Focusing on these KPIs transforms the legal function into a transparent, accountable and indispensable business partner.

### Cost per matter

Measuring the cost of handling specific matters or cases provides transparency into financial performance. For in-house legal teams, understanding cost per matter helps evaluate the allocation of resources, identify areas for budget optimisation, and justify expenditures while ensuring value-driven decision-making.

### Time to resolution (cycle time)

Cycle times reveal how quickly legal tasks such as contract reviews, dispute resolutions and regulatory matters are completed. Shorter cycle times for in-house teams translate to operational agility, reduced legal costs and improved responsiveness to requests.

### Client satisfaction (NPS)

Measuring client satisfaction using Net Promoter Scores (NPS) gauges the legal team’s effectiveness in meeting business and operational demands. Positive feedback indicates strong collaboration, alignment with organisational objectives and the ability to support departments efficiently, reinforcing the legal function as a trusted partner.

### Regulatory compliance rate

Legal teams by nature, are required to comply with laws, regulations and policies. High compliance rates are essential in mitigating risks and maintaining reputational standing, especially in regulated industries where oversight is critical and penalties are harsh.

### Volume and type of work

Tracking the number and categories of legal matters handled provides insights into workload distribution and team focus. For in-house legal teams, this data supports resource planning and helps prioritise high-value work, ensuring alignment with the company’s strategic goals while highlighting areas for optimising processes.

## How Plexus enables Legal teams to meet KPIs

Plexus is a powerful platform providing the tools and insights necessary for legal teams to pursue their North Star. The main benefits of using Plexus are:

**Streamlined workflow management**: Plexus [Contract Management](https://www.plexus.co/legal-suite/contract-management-software) and [Matter Management](https://www.plexus.co/legal-suite/matter-management-software) reduces cycle times of routine legal work and frees teams up to prioritise and handle high-value work more efficiently. Automated workflows and document generation also accelerate this.

**Data-driven insights**: In-platform analytics track workload volumes, helping teams optimise budgets and align resources with strategic and operational priorities.

**AI innovation**: Plexus AI-powered features such as Contract Summaries and [Contract Review](https://www.plexus.co/news/introducing-contract-review-powered-by-plexus-ai) further boost efficiency, demonstrating measurable ROI of legal work.

**Enhanced satisfaction**: Plexus improves collaboration with internal and external stakeholders, driving higher user engagement and satisfaction.

By integrating KPIs and agile practices through using Plexus, legal teams can align with business objectives, quantify their value and solidify their role as indispensable strategic partner. 

## The world's top GCs work with Plexus

As the global economy moves faster and becomes increasingly volatile, organisations must radically evolve their operating models to more dynamically identify and respond to opportunities and threats. Plexus helps leading GCs shift their organisational design, evolve their talent competencies and digitise their functions to deliver faster, most cost-effective and more agile legal support.

# Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) For Measuring Legal Operations Success

![Lawcadia logo](//files.lexology.com/images/lexology/firm/new/e01ae154-11dc-48a8-8e6c-4b1d587bc60b/W350/20201021032106.png)
![Sacha Kirk](//files.lexology.com/images/lexology/authorv2/squaretop/2c73dad0-7784-47fb-8517-1be800a432f0/W100/H100/20220503092222/photo.jpg "Lexology author: Sacha Kirk")

**In-house legal teams are increasingly expected to operate like a business function, delivering measurable value, optimising efficiency, and demonstrating strategic impact.**

However, assessing whether [legal operations](https://www.lawcadia.com/) initiatives are achieving their intended outcomes can be challenging without clear performance metrics.

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) provide a structured way to measure success, track improvements, and communicate legal’s value to the business. But which metrics matter most?

**Why KPIs Matter in Legal Operations**

Legal operations initiatives typically focus on efficiency, cost management, risk reduction, and stakeholder satisfaction.

Well-defined KPIs help:

The right KPIs will depend on the organisation’s goals, but a balanced approach includes efficiency, cost, risk, and service quality.

**Key Legal Operations KPIs**

**Efficiency [Metrics](https://www.lawcadia.com/reporting/)**

In-house legal teams must optimise how they handle work to ensure they can meet business demands effectively.

Efficiency KPIs focus on responsiveness, workload management, and process improvement.

**Cost [Metrics](https://www.lawcadia.com/reporting/)**

Managing legal spend is a core function of legal operations, ensuring the department delivers value while controlling costs.

Key financial KPIs include:

**Risk and Compliance [Metrics](https://www.lawcadia.com/reporting/)**

Legal teams play a key role in minimising regulatory and contractual risks.

Risk-related KPIs assess legal’s impact on business risk exposure.

**Stakeholder Satisfaction [Metrics](https://www.lawcadia.com/reporting/)**

A successful legal team isn’t just efficient, it must also be a trusted business partner.

Measuring stakeholder satisfaction helps assess whether legal delivers value to the wider organisation.

**Making KPIs Actionable**

Tracking KPIs is only valuable if the data is used to drive improvements.

In-house legal teams should:

**Conclusion**

KPIs are essential for transforming legal operations from a reactive function into a strategic business enabler. Rather than simply tracking activity, well-chosen KPIs provide meaningful insights that drive smarter decision-making, optimise resources, and align legal with business goals. When integrated into regular reporting and strategy discussions, these metrics help legal teams shift from being seen as a cost centre to a vital contributor to business success.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Track time saved on legal tasks through technology, leading to faster turnaround and improved productivity, ultimately enhancing overall operational efficiency.
Measure client satisfaction through surveys and feedback, utilizing Net Promoter Scores (NPS) to gauge service quality and repeat business potential.
Monitor the percentage of legal staff using new technology tools, ensuring effective integration and identifying areas needing additional training or support.
Analyze reductions in operational costs resulting from technology adoption, such as decreased reliance on external services and improved resource allocation.
